or hepatic. All these manifestations are generally
not serious.
DO CHILDREN RECOVER FROM THIS DISEASE?
These are chronic diseases that can
persist for a long time but to varying degrees. Children
and adults have long periods of remission
without signs of the disease. Most patients
have only moderate involvement well controlled
by treatment. These diseases are not life-threatening. In Crohn’s disease if a child should
ever need to undergo surgery this can lead to a prolonged remission but
it is not the definitive treatment of the disease.
In contrast, in UC, surgery can be
curative [(coloprotectomy (removal of the colon and
rectum)].
CAN MY CHILD BE MANAGED BY MY
FAMILY DOCTOR OR BY A SPECIALIST?
It is reasonable to consult a pediatrician specialized
in gastroenterology (intestinal specialist). This
specialist is trained to manage this kind
of problem and is connected with a team
capable of providing all the care and examinations
(endoscopy, surgery). Most Pediatrics departments will be able, either to take charge of the child, so
